title: Tangy Light Algerian Rhubarb Stir-fry
description: A diabetic-friendly solo dinner dish featuring tangy and light flavors of rhubarb cooked in a pressure cooker for quick and easy preparation.
instructions: Wash and thinly slice the rhubarb.
Chop the garlic into small pieces.
Heat a tablespoon of olive oil in a pressure cooker over medium heat.
Add the sliced rhubarb and sauté for about 3 minutes, stirring occasionally.
Add chopped garlic and red pepper flakes to the pan. Stir well for another minute.
Pour in the lemon juice and stir until fully incorporated.
Stir in the sugar and salt; taste and adjust seasoning if necessary.
Cook under high pressure for 15 minutes on a medium-high heat setting, then quick-release the pressure.
Serve hot with crusty bread or as a side dish.
